title: HuBMAP: Embedding Fixed Frozen OCT Samples
author: Marda Jorgensen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.basniede

steps.title: Prepare 1x PBS PBS is required...
steps.instruction: Prepare 1x PBS PBS is required to prepare reagents needed for this procedure. Dilute 10x PBS 1:10 in deionized water to make working 1x solution. PBS Phosphate Buffered Saline 10X Solution Fisher BioReagents Fisher Scientific Catalog #BP399-1

steps.title: Prepare a 4% paraformaldehyde solution
steps.instruction: Prepare a 4% paraformaldehyde solution. Paraformaldehyde is toxic. Use gloves, wear a lab coat and work in a fume hood.

steps.title: Open 2 -10mL ampules of 16% paraformaldehyde stock
steps.instruction: Open 2 -10mL ampules of 16% paraformaldehyde stock. Add contents to the 1x PBS in the sample cup, invert to mix. 20 mL, 16% Paraformaldehyde Stock

steps.title: Add 60ml of 100mM PBS to a sample cup
steps.instruction: Add 60ml of 100mM PBS to a sample cup. 60 mL, PBS Prepare containers to accomodate number and size of tissues using atleast a 1:20 tissue to fixative volume ratio.

steps.title: Collect tissue specimen from the designated common coordinate framework sites
steps.instruction: Collect tissue specimen from the designated common coordinate framework sites. (See case processing SOP diagrams) SOP Appendix for Spleen by Marda Jorgensen SOP Appendix for Thymus by Marda Jorgensen SOP Appendix for Lymph Node by Marda Jorgensen -

steps.title: Place tissue into pre-labelled cassettes maintaining orientation
steps.instruction: Place tissue into pre-labelled cassettes maintaining orientation.

steps.title: Transfer the tissue into a 120mL...
steps.instruction: Transfer the tissue into a 120mL specimen container, pre-filled with 80mL freshly prepared fixative (4% paraformaldehyde in PBS). Fix specimen for 24 hours at room temperature under mild agitation using a rocker. 24:00:00, In Fixative on Rocker Room temperature

steps.title: Drain fixative (collect as hazardous waste)...
steps.instruction: Drain fixative (collect as hazardous waste) and wash the tissue three (3) times for 10 minutes each in 1X PBS at a volume that generously covers. Invert several times during this process, or return to rocking platform. 00:30:00

steps.title: Infiltrate and cryoprotect the tissue with 30% sucrose
steps.instruction: Infiltrate and cryoprotect the tissue with 30% sucrose. 4 °C, in Sucrose/PBS Mixture 72:00:00

steps.title: Prepare a pre-labeled Cryomold Cryomold® (Histology,...
steps.instruction: Prepare a pre-labeled Cryomold Cryomold® (Histology, Tissue Tek®, 25608-916, 25mm x 20 mm x 5mm, https://us.vwr.com/store/product/4639407/tissue-tek-cryomold-molds-adapters-sakura-finetek ) and fill it half way with OCT compound. Tissue-Tek® O.C.T. Compound, Sakura® Finetek VWR International (Avantor) Catalog #25608-930

steps.title: Remove the tissue from the cassette...
steps.instruction: Remove the tissue from the cassette using foceps and quickly touch the tissue to a Kimwipe to remove external sucrose droplet from tissue surface.

steps.title: Place the tissue into the OCT-containing...
steps.instruction: Place the tissue into the OCT-containing Cryomold, maintaining original tissue orientation.

steps.title: Using forceps, push the tissue lightly...
steps.instruction: Using forceps, push the tissue lightly to the bottom of the Cryomold to secure it.

steps.title: Add additional OCT to cover the...
steps.instruction: Add additional OCT to cover the tissue completely and fill the Cryomold. Allow the tissue to equilibrate in the mold for ten (10) minutes. 00:10:00, In Cryomold

steps.title: Prepare dry ice/methylbutane slurry for freezing OCT blocks
steps.instruction: Prepare dry ice/methylbutane slurry for freezing OCT blocks.

steps.title: Freeze the tissue in the Cryomold...
steps.instruction: Freeze the tissue in the Cryomold by resting it on the surface of the methylbutane slurry. As the OCT inside the Cryomold begins to freeze, lightly push the tissue into the bottom of the mold one last time.

steps.title: Place 1-2 inches of dry ice...
steps.instruction: Place 1-2 inches of dry ice pellets into the bolltom of an ice bucket or styrofoam box.

steps.title: Add enough 2-methylbutane solution to cover...
steps.instruction: Add enough 2-methylbutane solution to cover the dry ice by roughly 5mm .

steps.title: When the OCT Cryomold is completely...
steps.instruction: When the OCT Cryomold is completely frozen and opaque, wrap the mold containing the tissue in a pre-labeled aluminum foil square, and store at -80° C in a freezer rack box. Enter block location in Freezer Log for future retrieval

steps.title: Prepare 30% sucrose solution
steps.instruction: Prepare 30% sucrose solution

steps.title: Label containers, solution expires one week after being made
steps.instruction: Label containers, solution expires one week after being made. Store at 4° C if not immediately used.

steps.title: Combine 300g Sucrose with 1 liter of 1X PBS
steps.instruction: Combine 300g Sucrose with 1 liter of 1X PBS. Add stir bar and mix on a stir plate until dissolved. 300 g, sucrose 1 L, 1x PBS

steps.title: Store at 4° C in labeled container
steps.instruction: Store at 4° C in labeled container. Check for contamination prior to use, no expiation date.

steps.title: Combine equal volumes of 30% sucrose...
steps.instruction: Combine equal volumes of 30% sucrose and 1x PBS to form 15% Sucrose. Replace PBS wash with 15% sucrose and place at 4° C for 8 hours

steps.title: Drain 15% sucrose and replace with 30% sucrose
steps.instruction: Drain 15% sucrose and replace with 30% sucrose. Equillibrate tissue 4° C for at least 48 hours. Tissue is stable in sucrose for at least one week.

steps.title: Place lid on freezing chamber and allow methylbutane to chill
steps.instruction: Place lid on freezing chamber and allow methylbutane to chill. Chamber is ready when fog dissipates and bottom of bucket becomes visible.
